signals.title: USD/JPY — Fed Hawkishness + BoJ Hike + Carry + Positioning
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.41, surging to near 2024 highs following the Fed's hawkish policy update, which held rates at 3.75% and stripped cut-bias language. This reinforces the significant +290bp carry advantage for USD (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%). Despite the BoJ's recent rate hike to 1.0% and a 'Hike' market bias, JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (week ending Jun 9) indicates extreme crowded speculative short positioning against a hawkish BoJ. This creates significant short-squeeze risk if the BoJ signals further tightening or intervenes. The 160.00 level has been breached, raising intervention risk. Watch for BoJ verbal warnings or direct action above 161.50.
evidence: JPY COT LF net: -115,036 (week ending Jun 9)

signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + BoE Hold + Options Skew
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3208, with GBP as the weakest G10 currency this week (avg -0.818% vs peers) while USD is the strongest (avg +1.056%). The 1.874% leader-to-laggard spread highlights significant bilateral divergence. The BoE held rates at 3.75% (Market bias: Hold), contrasting with the hawkish Fed. Options market shows a moderate put skew (25d RR at -0.76), indicating demand for GBP downside protection. This combination of G10 underperformance, neutral BoE stance, and options hedging suggests continued GBP weakness. Watch for any shift in BoE forward guidance or UK economic data to alleviate pressure.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5754. COT LF net short −22,354 (week ending Jun 9) indicates speculative positioning is heavily short NZD, while the RBNZ has a 'Hike' market bias. This divergence creates short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ delivers or signals a hike at its next meeting. Further compounding this, retail sentiment shows an extreme 93% long bias, diverging from institutional shorts. If RBNZ delivers a hawkish surprise, a crowded spec short unwind combined with retail capitulation is likely to target 0.5800+. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 8 Jul is the key catalyst.
evidence: NZD COT LF net: -22,354 (week ending Jun 9)

signals.title: USD/CHF — Elevated Vol Premium + Carry + Geopolitical
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.8043, rallying to a new yearly high. The pair benefits from a substantial +375bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%). Options market is pricing an elevated vol premium (IV 14.0% vs HV30 6.9% — VRP +7.1%), indicating strong demand for USD upside protection. The US and Iran formally signing a peace memorandum and reopening the Strait of Hormuz has lifted stocks, but the underlying geopolitical tensions and safe-haven demand for USD persist. The SNB's next meeting on 18 Jun (today) is on hold, reinforcing the carry differential. Watch for any shift in global risk sentiment or SNB commentary.
